rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Answer Deposition Questions; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANT THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Answer Deposition Questions; Request For Monetary Sanctions. The courts adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Thornley as follows: GRANTED in part, DENIED in part. Discovery referee's ruling of June 11, 2012 is affirmed. Motion denied with respect to deposition questions 6, 7, 10 and 17; denied with respect to questions 23-28 with leave to rephrase; and granted with respect to the remaining questions. David Yang ordered to pay sanctions of $1,260 within 15 days. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE WASHINGTON CLARK III VS. SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. A hearing is required. Plaintiff will be appearing telephonically. On the merits, the motion is granted. Defendant shifted its burden to show that Plaintiff failed to file the requisite government claim under Gov. Code §§ 905, 905.1, 905.2, and 911.2(a) before filing this suit. Plaintiff concedes in his letter to the Court that he did not file such claim. The Court denies Plaintiff's request for a continuance because there is nothing that would change the facts of this case which require that the motion be granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Question S From Deft Prime Pacific Investments, Inc And Kamran Ardebilchi; Request For Sanctions, Costs And Fees Pursuant To Ccp 2023.010 (D,E,F,I) 2023.020, 2025.480 (A,E,F)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 7, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs LOUIS GAMACHE, PAUL GARRARD, DANIELLE MCGEE's Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Questions From Defendant Prime Pacific Investments, Inc. And Kamran Ardebilchi; Request For Sanctions, Costs And Fees Pursuant To CCP 2023.010 (D,E,F,I) 2023.020, 2025.480 (A,E,F). Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHINGLE SPRINGS TRIBAL GAMING AUTHORITY et al VS. HOEM & ASSOCIATES, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012, Line 4, CROSS DEFENDANT CUNNINGHAM GROUP ARCHITECTURE, P.A.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied. Cross-Defendant has pointed to no legal authority that requires Cross-Complainant to file a new Certificate of Merit when it filed its First Amended Cross-Complaint. Cross-Complainant's objections to the King Declaration are sustained. In ruling on a demurrer, the Court can only refer to matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (See Arce v. Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc. (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 471.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RIMMA BOROKHOV et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order Quashng Deposition Supoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 7, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANTs ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, WILLIAM HUTTON, RAYMOND BROWN's Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: It is denied as to all records sought for cases filed from January 1, 2004, through the present. It is granted as to all records sought for cases filed before January 1, 2004. This is without prejudice, one way or the other, to plaintiffs seeking to introduce evidence of any of the cases, regardless of when filed, at trial. It is also without prejudice, one way or the other, to plaintiffs issuing a new subpoena for records for cases filed before January 1, 2004, based on newly discovered evidence. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RIMMA BOROKHOV et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Compel Pltf'S Further Written Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Form Interrogatrories; And To Compel Pltf'S Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 7, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT ANASON PAK's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Further Written Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Form Interrogatrories; And To Compel Plaintiffs' Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Pak's motion to compel further responses to document requests and interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part. It is granted as follows in part as to Request 25 of the Requests for Production of Documents per plaintiffs' proposals: Plaintiffs shall produce all documents reflecting or referring to: (1) their emotional distress resulting from defendants' alleged wrongful conduct; and (2) their emotional distress suffered from any source in the one year prior to the filing of the complaint in this litigation. Defendant Pak's' motion to compel as to Request 25 is otherwise denied. The request on the state of the record provided in this motion is overbroad and invades privacy. The denial is without prejudice, one way or the other, to defendant making new requests for documents related to other sources of emotional distress that are for more specific documents and/or are based on newly discovered evidence. Plaintiffs shall amend their response to Request 25 to reflect that they have produced these documents. Plaintiffs shall comply with this order within 20 days of when they are served with the order. The court denies defendant's motion to compel as to Request 5. The request is not stated with sufficient particularity. The court denies defendant's motion as to all other document requests and interrogatories. The motion to compel as to them is moot since the parties have resolved them. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E. BUTLER VS. LAUREEN PARAGUYA DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANT LAUREEN PARAGUYA'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The fraud/ conspiracy and negligent infliction of emotional distress causes of action are adequately pled with the necessary specificity. The Court considers conspiracy a part of the general charging allegations. It is therefore sufficient that the Third Amended Complaint alleges the named defendants committed the acts constituting the conspiracy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: AARON FERGUSON et al VS. W. B. COYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANTS W. COYLE, TELEGRAPH HILL PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled their alter ego allegations. The third cause of action is not duplicative of the second cause of action and therefore is not improperly included in the Third Amended Complaint. The Prayer for Disgorgement of Commissions is properly pled because Plaintiffs are entitled to seek monetary damages and equitable relief in the alternative. (See Ripling v. Sup.Ct. (Norton) (1952) 112 Cal.App.2d 399, 408.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: AARON FERGUSON et al VS. W. B. COYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANT THP-SF, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted pursuant to CCP § 473(b). Relief under CCP § 473.5 is inappropriate because Defendant failed to show that it lacked actual notice of this suit. Although there is scant evidence showing mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect, CCP § 473(b) is applied liberally where the party seeking relief moves diligently and there is no prejudice to the party opposing the motion. (See Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233-234.) Defendant has ten days to file a responsive pleading. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012, Line 14, RESPONDENT IGOR PALAGINS MOTION To Dismiss Appeal Labor Commissioner Order. Granted in part. Under the clear language of Labor Code § 98.2(b), as amended in 2010, Defendant must post a bond before filing an appeal. Defendant is given ten days to post a bond in the amount of $34,359.32. If the bond is not posted by 4pm on August 17, 2012 Plaintiff may appear ex parte and have this action dismissed.
